title: uglabdbm
text: uglabdbm Get a list of all the model entities in the component. Explain this code short uglabdbm (unsigned char*Buffer); Copy Parameters Buffer (Output) The comma-separated list of entities. This buffer must be large enough to contain the data. Return Values 0—this function always returns zero. Use Library: yrtl Description uglabdbm gets the names of all the model entities in the calling component, and places them in buffer , separated by commas. The buffer will be zero-terminated. Entities that are painted only on the component, but not defined in the application model (so-called ‘dummy entities’), are not included in the list. CAUTION: This function, unlike most functions that get data, does not have a max parameter to specify the maximum number of bytes returned. You must ensure that your buffer is large enough to hold all the names. If not, your application is likely to crash. The following example prints the list of model entities in the component to the message frame. (The size of 2000 bytes for the buffer is arbitrary, but will probably be enough in most cases). Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> EXPORT void GETENTNAMES () { char buf[2000]; uglabdbm(buf); u_printf("Model entities in the component are : %s\n:, buf); } Copy Related Links uflabdbm Get a list of the names of all database fields in an entity.
